highlights.text: I wrote last Friday August 1st at 4pm: “The bull case Monday is that ES can do some back-testing of the zones we broke down yesterday and today.
I wrote Thursday at 4pm (when were 6360s): “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
I call this “elevator down” and it sees ES lose many supports rapidly.
This turns sentiment bearish, and top-callers appear 2) All “elevator down” sells in ES resolve with a violent short squeeze.
These short squeezes are caused by my core setup: The Failed Breakdown.
A Failed Breakdown is when price sweeps a low, traps shorts, then recovers.
After selling 230 points last week, on Friday August 1st in the afternoon we saw a massive Failed Breakdown where ES swept a major low at 6245 then recovered, getting us long.
Then last Thursday, ES saw an “elevator down” sell, reversing from 6420s down to 6333.
Sentiment was bearish here, but we were longing, and last Thursday at around 2:50PM ES got a quality Failed Breakdown of a shelf of lows at 6344.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive review into the Failed Breakdowns we saw on Wednesday and Thursday last week each which gave us a nearly 100 point leg up (these were given in advance).

post_title: CPI Day Tomorrow. Will It Derail, Or Accelerate The Current Rally? August 12th Plan
